summary: Domain transformations import rendering.formulas, violating the intended hexagonal boundary.
detail: Rohliste marks D-001 as the symptom and IR-002 as the fix location for the same layer-boundary issue.
source_detail: Rohliste marks D-001 as the symptom and IR-002 as the fix location for the same layer-boundary issue.
fix_comment: Formula intent was moved from rendering to core and the domain/core -> rendering/io_backends boundary is now guard-protected. REVIEW-002 marks D-001 + IR-002 resolved and guarded.
current_assessment: Formula intent was moved from rendering to core and the domain/core -> rendering/io_backends boundary is now guard-protected. REVIEW-002 marks D-001 + IR-002 resolved and guarded.
description: This finding captured a critical layer-boundary violation: code in the domain transformations layer was directly importing from rendering.formulas, which broke the intended hexagonal architecture separating domain logic from rendering concerns. It mattered because such imports create tight coupling that makes the domain layer untestable in isolation and fragile to rendering-side changes. Based on the existing fields this is a resolved historical item: status is 'done' and current_relevance is 'historical'. The fix moved formula intent from rendering into core and added a guard on the domain/core → rendering/io_backends boundary. For further context check fix_evidence_path (FTR-REVIEW-001-FORMULAS-CORE-MOVE-P3 and REVIEW-002-FINDINGS) and the related finding FIN-REVIEW-001-IR-002.

source_detail: Clustered with FK naming overlap, backward-compatibility residue, and FK step-name inconsistency.
fix_comment: The original domain/fk move target was superseded by the later FK-helper lifecycle redesign: primitives became policy-driven, FK helper runtime moved to domain-owned packages, and core.fk is retained deliberately as leaf/wire-format logic. This is not a literal domain/fk extraction, but the coupling risk is no longer the same issue.
current_assessment: The original domain/fk move target was superseded by the later FK-helper lifecycle redesign: primitives became policy-driven, FK helper runtime moved to domain-owned packages, and core.fk is retained deliberately as leaf/wire-format logic. This is not a literal domain/fk extraction, but the coupling risk is no longer the same issue.
description: This finding flagged that core/fk.py was the home for FK naming conventions and helper primitives that conceptually belong in the domain layer, not the lower-level core layer. The concern was that placing domain-owned logic in core creates an upward dependency and muddies ownership. Status is 'superseded' and current_relevance is 'historical': the fix path turned out not to be a literal extraction but a lifecycle redesign — FK helpers became policy-driven, their runtime moved to domain-owned packages, and core.fk was intentionally retained as leaf/wire-format logic. The original coupling risk is therefore no longer the same issue. Check fix_evidence_path (FTR-FK-HELPER-DOMAIN-EXTRACTION, FTR-FK-HELPERS-POLICY-DRIVEN-PRIMITIVES-P5, fk_helper_consolidation_closure, REVIEW-002) and related findings D-003, D-009, IR-011.

description: The bug BUG-CROSS-CARRIER-META-ROUNDTRIP exposed a class-wide problem: the registry classifies meta entries at the root level (canonical_meta, derived_operational_view, carrier_artifact, read_path_hint) but the persistence boundary had no policy for sub-key facets within a canonical-classified root. This allowed render-time materializations to survive into _meta.yaml sidecars, which were then consumed by a different backend. Symptom-narrow (one XLSX failure) but class-wide (any classification weakness in any facet). The E2-narrow repair closed the three most clear-cut Resolution facets.

description: The resolved sub-key under legend_blocks contains layout coordinates (left column index, top row index, n_rows, n_cols, sheet name, frame name) computed by _resolve_legend_position at line ~246 of layout_composer.py. These are the output of composer math on current pipeline state, not declarative user intent. They survive recomputation unchanged if the pipeline reruns on the same inputs, making them Resolution state. Persisting them created cross-carrier risk when a sidecar from backend A was consumed by backend B. Pruned in d816d9a as part of the E2-narrow repair.

description: The fixture contained helper_columns: [title] entries for several sheets where 'title' does not match helper_prefix: '_'. Under Canonical-vs-Carrier this looked like reader-side observation. Under Intent-vs-Resolution the test is different: was the list declared or resolved? That question cannot be answered from the value alone. Two different producers can emit identical shapes. The correct place to answer declared-vs-resolved is the producer audit owned by FTR-FK-HELPER-POLICY-LIFECYCLE-P4A and FTR-DERIVED-PROVENANCE-LIFECYCLE-P4A. The correct cases (_places_name, _regions_name) are Intent regardless of framing.

description: The addendum to the canonical meta boundary assessment introduced the Intent-vs-Resolution framing. Under this lens, frame_lifecycle.derived_from and frame_lifecycle.produced_by are unambiguous Resolution: a pipeline step recording which other step produced this frame on this run is the pipeline reporting on itself. The value is recomputed on the next run. Persisting it creates stale-state risk if pipeline configuration changes between save and re-load. The Canonical-vs-Carrier framing could not surface this because it focuses on who wrote the value (a pipeline step is 'internal'), not on the nature of the value. A producer/consumer audit is needed before pruning to avoid breaking any current readers.

summary: Intent-vs-Resolution is a more stable persistence boundary rule than Canonical-vs-Carrier: it focuses on the nature of the value (declarative wish vs. materialized consequence) rather than the producer, allowing new producers to apply the rule locally without consulting the registry.

description: The addendum to the assessment recast the E2 repair candidates under Intent-vs-Resolution rather than Canonical-vs-Carrier. The new rule: 'the persistence boundary preserves Intent (declarative wish, stable across re-runs on the same inputs) and drops Resolution (materialized from current data, current layout, or current pipeline state); Provenance rides alongside Intent and is neither.' This rule allows a new producer to apply it locally by asking one question: 'does this value survive a clean re-run unchanged?' — without needing to consult the registry. The Canonical-vs-Carrier framing required a registry lookup. Accepted as the project's persistence boundary principle going forward.

description: The meta flattening hypothesis stated that _meta had gradually shifted from a pragmatic sidecar carrier into a central semantic ontology: a dozen lifecycle/ownership questions created a large combinatorial space of possible classifications. The preferred direction was: do not classify every metadata item into a global ontology; for each feature-local datum, make only the operational decisions that matter (keep, transport, persist, ignore, validate, clean up, or remove). This hypothesis was validated and applied through the FK-helper functional model (d4a63f6) and the subsequent consolidation arc. Related to FIN-TOPIC-META-MFRG-001 which captured an earlier seed version of the same hypothesis.

summary: In an owner-controlled, consumer-few project with a strong test suite, the default risk balance for legacy metadata should be 'simplify unless current code, tests, or consumers prove the layer is still needed' — not 'keep unless removal is proven safe'.

description: The hypothesis inverted the default risk posture for legacy metadata cleanup. The traditional stance ('keep unless removal is proven safe') is appropriate for a mature product with unknown external users and uncontrolled historical data. The project situation differs: owner-controlled, few and controlled consumers, deliberate migration possible, strong safety nets (test suite and consumer roundtrips). The new default ('simplify unless current code proves the layer is needed') enabled the FK-helper v1 per-target dict retirement and other cleanups that would have been blocked under the prior posture.

description: The hypothesis identified FK Helpers as carrying seven historical layers: v2 relation policy, schema_version marker, residual v1 per-target shape, runtime helper provenance (_meta.derived), workbook-view/sheet-local declarations, produced_by markers, and registry lifecycle language. The working suspicion was not that all of these are wrong but that several existed because FK helpers survived multiple architecture migrations rather than because current runtime behavior required them. The proposed approach — code-first falsification inventory before any cleanup — was the pilot for the meta-flattening direction. Applied successfully in the FK-helper consolidation arc.
